mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, consumer spending power contracts and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed cases during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 200.0 index level forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 200.0 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 110.0 index threshold signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where INDPRO drops below 110.0.
